forecasts.reasoning: Beto O'Rourke is actively holding town halls in Texas (via his group Powered by People) and recently teased that he would run for the Senate 'if Texans want him to'. However, the July 1, 2025 deadline constraint significantly depresses the probability of a Yes resolution. On May 1, Texas Monthly quoted O'Rourke saying it was 'too early to make a definitive proclamation about 2026 or 2028.' Additionally, O'Rourke benefits from delaying a formal announcement: remaining a non-candidate allows him to raise funds for his PAC without strict FEC limits, and his near-universal name ID in Texas means he doesn't need a year-and-a-half runway (for his 2022 gubernatorial bid, he waited until November 2021 to declare). While he remains a likely contender for the race, the odds of an official announcement in the very brief ~5-week window before July 1 are low.

Following the intense military escalation in May 2025 (Operation Sindoor), a US-brokered ceasefire was established on May 10. While there were immediate minor violations, recent reports (as of May 21) indicate the ceasefire 'has held; fragile, uneasy but still unbroken.' The involvement of high-level US mediation, including plans for talks at a neutral site, introduces strong diplomatic pressure to maintain the pause in hostilities over the short term. Because the initial retaliatory aims for the April Pahalgam attack were largely met by the May 7 strikes, an unprovoked resumption of kinetic strikes hitting 'Pakistan proper' (Punjab, Sindh, KPK, Baluchistan) specifically in June is unlikely unless triggered by a new major terrorist event. Some risk remains due to the proximity of the International Border to Punjab and India's stated 'new normal' doctrine, placing the probability at around 16%.

The probability of a pilot report of a UFO from Africa occurring in the specific month of June 2025 and being posted on the NUFORC pilot index by July 1, 2025 is extraordinarily low. Our search shows that the specific NUFORC pilot reporting index `nuforc.org/subndx/?id=x` historically contains only 33 entries spanning 1966 to 2014, with exactly zero originating from the African continent. This indicates that pilot reports on this specific index are phenomenally rare (less than 1 globally per year on average). The geographic filter (Africa), the requirement for the reporter to be a pilot, the narrow 1-month sighting window (June 2025), and the extremely tight reporting deadline (must be posted to NUFORC by July 1st, despite their notorious batch-updating lag) stack together to make this outcome practically impossible outside of a highly coordinated anomalous event. There is no evidence of such an event or organized reporting campaign. I assign the minimum probability bound of 0.02.

Rippling just closed a $450 million Series G funding round at a $16.8 billion valuation on May 9, 2025. In coverage of this round, CEO Parker Conrad explicitly stated that the company is not planning an IPO in the near future and will instead use annual tender offers to provide liquidity to employees. Given that this explicit denial of IPO plans was made less than two weeks before the forecast date, and the resolution date is only 40 days away, it is virtually impossible that Rippling will reverse course and announce an IPO before July 1, 2025. I am submitting the minimum allowed probability of 0.02.

The government of Mali recently issued a decree on May 13, 2025, strictly dissolving all political parties and political organizations. This action followed a suspension of political activities on May 7 aimed at preempting protests, and a recommendation by a national dialogue forum to extend junta leader Assimi Goita's rule for five more years without an election. Since the decree, opposition figures have reportedly been arrested or "disappeared," and political space has been radically shut down. The junta previously implemented a temporary suspension in early 2024 which took three months to lift; a formal dissolution is a much more permanent and drastic step, representing a calculated consolidation of power. Given that the forecast horizon (July 1, 2025) is less than six weeks away from the current date, reversing such a newly implemented, core strategic decision is extraordinarily unlikely absent a complete regime collapse, for which there are currently no immediate signs. Moreover, Mali has distanced itself from regional bodies like ECOWAS, limiting external pressure. Therefore, the probability of reversing the decree by July 1 is estimated at the floor of 2%.

Databricks CEO Ali Ghodsi stated in late 2024 that it was "dumb to IPO" and they were waiting until at least 2025. By January 2025, Databricks raised an unprecedented $10B-$15B in equity and debt financing, which multiple reports cited as a move specifically designed to delay an IPO until market conditions are optimal. There have been no reports in 2025 of Databricks hiring investment bankers or underwriters for a near-term IPO, nor have there been credible rumors of a confidential S-1 filing. An IPO roadmap typically involves hiring bankers several months in advance, followed by a confidential S-1 submission before any public filing. Given less than six weeks remain until the July 1, 2025 deadline and Databricks is extremely well-capitalized, the probability of them announcing an IPO by the target date is exceptionally low.

Extensive searches of x.com and general news databases for 'diversity visa' alongside Elon Musk and Donald Trump yielded no evidence that either has tweeted this exact phrase recently. Furthermore, specific searches for mentions involving replies to the @fentasyl account or referencing tweets from 2025 returned no relevant hits. While there is current discussion around visas (e.g., H-1B and a 'Gold Card Visa' mentioned by Musk), the specific phrase 'diversity visa' hasn't been posted by either of these two individuals as of May 22, 2025. Given the April 1, 2025 date in the question title has already passed (resolving to NO for that period), and even if the July 1, 2025 date in the resolution criteria is the true deadline, the probability of them spontaneously tweeting this exacting phrase in the ~40 remaining days is extremely low. There is a small residual chance if Trump actively pushes to eliminate the Diversity Visa Lottery and tweets about it, but the base rate of using this exact phrase on X remains minimal.

Beto O'Rourke recently (late April/early May 2025) started testing the waters for a 2026 Senate run, stating he will run 'if Texans want him to'. This significantly increases the base likelihood of him running. However, the resolution deadline is July 1, 2025, which is only 40 days from the forecast date. While Senate campaigns often launch in the spring or summer of the off-year (Colin Allred announced for 2024 in May 2023), Beto waited until November 2021 to announce his 2022 gubernatorial run. Furthermore, Colin Allred is reportedly seriously considering another run for the seat and is currently polling favorably, which could cause Beto to either defer to him or wait to consolidate support before declaring, further delaying any announcement. Considering the tight 40-day window, the chance of him deferring/not running due to Allred, and his potential to wait until the fall to announce, the probability of an official announcement before July 1 is estimated at 25%.

On May 9, 2025, Rippling announced a $450 million funding round at a $16.8 billion valuation. In conjunction with this announcement, CEO Parker Conrad explicitly stated that an IPO is "not imminent" and the company is "not planning for an IPO in the near future." He explained that while public markets currently favor profitability and slower growth, Rippling is prioritizing high growth (currently >30% YoY) and is not fixated on profitability right now. Furthermore, a portion of the raised capital is being used for a $200 million secondary tender offer to provide liquidity to current and former employees, which mitigates one of the primary pressures to go public. Given these explicit statements from the CEO less than two months before the July 1, 2025 resolution date, the probability of an IPO announcement or S-1 filing in this timeframe is effectively zero.

The base rate for a company that just raised $10B privately in January 2025 to file for an IPO within the next five months is extremely low. There are only about 40 days left until the resolution date (July 1, 2025), and there have been no credible recent rumors that Databricks has hired investment bankers or confidentially submitted an S-1, which are typical precursors that leak to the press. Over the past six months, the CEO has continuously emphasized that the company has no urgent need to go public, explicitly stating that a potential IPO could happen in the second half of 2025 or even in 2026 at the earliest. In the absence of any indications of active IPO preparation, the probability of an SEC Form S-1 filing or a formal announcement by July 1, 2025, is as close to zero as possible.

The base rate for NUFORC sightings from the African continent is extremely low, as NUFORC is heavily US/Canada-focused. The probability of an event meeting three rare criteria simultaneously—(1) occurring in Africa, (2) being submitted specifically by a pilot, and (3) happening exactly during the single month of June 2025—is practically negligible. While one such anomaly was recorded in January 2025 (in a report seemingly containing geographical inconsistencies like referencing a Canadian military base in Rwanda), this does not establish a trend or significantly raise the base rate for any given future month. Because of this, my estimate is at the lowest permitted bound (0.02).

In late April/early May 2025, a significant military conflict erupted between India and Pakistan following the April 22 Pahalgam terror attack. India launched 'Operation Sindhoor' which included airstrikes. However, on May 10, a ceasefire heavily brokered by the US (specifically Donald Trump) went into effect. As of late May, this ceasefire is holding; civilian airports have reopened and markets have stabilized, signaling a real de-escalation rather than a brief tactical pause. 

While Prime Minister Modi has publicly called the ceasefire a 'pause' and claimed Operation Sindhoor is 'still continuing', this rhetoric is overwhelmingly likely designed for domestic consumption to placate nationalist anger from his political base, who feel India did not achieve a decisive victory. Actually resuming large-scale kinetic strikes weeks after a major US-mediated de-escalation would carry massive diplomatic costs and risk unmanageable escalation. 

Furthermore, the resolution criteria are strict: the kinetic attack must occur within Pakistan proper (Sindh, Baluchistan, Punjab, or KP), explicitly excluding the Line of Control and Pakistan-occupied Kashmir (PoK) where border skirmishes typically happen when a ceasefire frays. A strike deep into Pakistan's sovereign provinces is an extreme measure (e.g., Balakot in 2019 was historically unprecedented). The likelihood of India executing such a highly escalatory strike in June, absent a massive new provocation, is very low.

On May 13, 2025, Mali's transitional military government enacted a presidential decree formally dissolving all political parties. This action is part of a deliberate and broader scheme to consolidate power, following national consultations recommending a renewable five-year presidential term for junta leader Assimi Goïta. The timeline for a reversal before July 1, 2025, is exceptionally short (less than 6 weeks). Regimes rarely reverse major power-consolidating decrees so quickly unless forced by existential pressure. While an opposition coalition has protested, these demonstrations took place prior to the formal decree and failed to deter the junta. Instead, authorities responded by arresting opposition figures and suppressing media coverage. Furthermore, Mali's withdrawal from ECOWAS and alignment with fellow junta-led nations (Niger, Burkina Faso) significantly decreases its vulnerability to regional diplomatic leverage. Given the regime's demonstrated willingness to use repression and the lack of overwhelming external or internal forces capable of coercing an immediate reversal, the probability of the decree being overturned by July 2025 is extremely low.

Neither Donald Trump nor Elon Musk have tweeted the exact phrase "diversity visa" as of May 22, 2025. Comprehensive searches of their respective X accounts and recent news coverage show that their immigration discussions have heavily focused on H-1B visas and border control, with no mention of the diversity visa. Although Trump aggressively criticized the program during his first term (and an account Musk follows tweeted about it in February 2025), neither has used the exact phrase recently. With only about 1.5 months remaining until the July 1, 2025 resolution date, and no clear legislative push currently making headlines regarding the diversity visa, the likelihood of a spontaneous exact-phrase tweet is very low.
